reference_impl: jeremylhour/alternative-synthetic-control-sparsity R, run live on mlsynth's exact design matrix (raw pre-means of loginc/p_cig/pct15-24/pc_beer + cigsale lags 1975/1980/1988), c_cal=0.03, c_ort=0.3
notes: California treated 1989. R run on mlsynth's exported X/d/Y so the only difference is the calibration optimizer (R OWL-QN vs mlsynth proximal-gradient); ATT path matches to ~0.02 packs, SEs to ~0.002. BEAST targets the sparse informative covariate regime; on the over-rich augmented set the balancing degenerates (sum(W) != 1) and both R and Python fail -- guarded by balance_tol.
